LanguageData.Megascarab.description: A massive ant engineered for strength and resilience. Giant ants are known for their powerful mandibles and hive loyalty.
LanguageData.Megascarab.label: giant worker ant
LanguageData.Megaspider.description: The most aggressive of the giant ants. These creatures defend their hive with relentless ferocity and powerful jaws.
LanguageData.Megaspider.label: giant soldier ant
LanguageData.Spelopede.description: A giant ant adapted for underground life. Its tough exoskeleton and digging ability make it a formidable tunnel dweller.
LanguageData.Spelopede.label: giant ant
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireAnt.description: A stasis cocoon containing a fire ant.
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireAnt.label: fire ant cocoon
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireSoldierAnt.description: A stasis cocoon containing a fire soldier ant.
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireSoldierAnt.label: fire soldier ant cocoon
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireWorkerAnt.description: A stasis cocoon containing a fire worker ant.
LanguageData.Arktos_CocoonFireWorkerAnt.label: fire worker ant cocoon
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAnt.description: A fire ant adapted for underground life. Its digging claws and hive coordination make it a formidable tunnel dweller.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAnt.label: fire ant
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAnt.tools.0.label: head claw
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAnt.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntDrone.description: A winged fire ant bred for mating flights and scouting. On its own it is fragile, but swarms of drones are still dangerous.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntDrone.label: fire ant drone
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntDrone.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntDrone.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntHive.description: A hive of giant fire ants. If activated, it will spawn additional fire ants and hives over time, as well as valuable insect jelly.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntHive.label: fire ant hive
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntLarva.description: The juvenile stage of a fire ant. Fire ant larvae are nurtured by workers and mature into the hive's next caste.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntLarva.label: fire ant larva
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntLarva.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntLarva.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntQueen.description: The queen of a fire ant megahive. She anchors the colony, lays the next generation, and turns any nest she survives in into a larger threat.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntQueen.label: fire ant queen
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntQueen.tools.0.label: head claw
LanguageData.Arktos_FireAntQueen.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Arktos_FireSoldierAnt.description: The most aggressive of the fire ants. These creatures defend their hive with relentless ferocity, thick chitin, and powerful jaws.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireSoldierAnt.label: fire soldier ant
LanguageData.Arktos_FireSoldierAnt.tools.0.label: head claw
LanguageData.Arktos_FireSoldierAnt.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Arktos_FireWorkerAnt.description: A massive fire ant engineered for strength and resilience. Fire worker ants are fiercely territorial and maintain their hive with absolute loyalty.
LanguageData.Arktos_FireWorkerAnt.label: fire worker ant
LanguageData.Arktos_FireWorkerAnt.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.Arktos_FireWorkerAnt.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.Fish_Salmon.description: A migratory fish with dark scales and rich flesh, common where cold rivers meet the sea.
LanguageData.Fish_Salmon.label: smoky salmon
LanguageData.Fish_Bass.description: A common freshwater predator known for sudden strikes and mild-tasting meat.
LanguageData.Fish_Bass.label: chain pickerel
LanguageData.Fish_Tilapia.description: A hardy freshwater fish found in warm rivers and ponds, often thriving where cleaner species cannot.
LanguageData.Fish_Tilapia.label: withered radeye
LanguageData.Fish_Cod.description: A coldwater coastal fish with thick scales and a stiff dorsal ridge, valued for its firm white flesh.
LanguageData.Fish_Cod.label: armored spinefish
LanguageData.Fish_Bluefish.description: A fast coastal hunter that chases bait schools in rough weather and open surf.
LanguageData.Fish_Bluefish.label: stormswimmer
LanguageData.Fish_Guppy.description: A small freshwater species prized for its unusual frilled gills; edible, but too small to provide much meat.
LanguageData.Fish_Guppy.label: axolotl
LanguageData.Fish_Frostfish.description: A large coldwater fish with pale flesh and a heavy body, usually found in deep glacier-fed rivers.
LanguageData.Fish_Frostfish.label: potbelly kelt
LanguageData.Fish_Catfish.description: A large whiskered bottom-feeder that prowls muddy channels and flooded ruins after dark.
LanguageData.Fish_Catfish.label: bloodwhisker
LanguageData.Fish_Piranha.description: A small but aggressive freshwater fish with sharp teeth and powerful jaws, often caught as a trophy species.
LanguageData.Fish_Piranha.label: kanawha piranha
LanguageData.Fish_Dogfish.description: A small shark-like coastal fish with rough skin and sharp teeth, common along rocky shorelines.
LanguageData.Fish_Dogfish.label: sawgill
LanguageData.Fish_Marlin.description: A huge open-water hunter with a spear-like snout, prized as a dangerous trophy catch.
LanguageData.Fish_Marlin.label: deathjaw
LanguageData.Fish_Tuna.description: A large migratory fish from warm seas, known for oily flesh and strong sustained runs.
LanguageData.Fish_Tuna.label: bluefin zapper
LanguageData.Fish_Flounder.description: A flat coastal fish that buries itself in silt and ambushes prey along the seafloor.
LanguageData.Fish_Flounder.label: ashen ambusher
LanguageData.Fish_Toxfish.description: A polluted-water mutant fish. Unsafe raw, but still usable in cooked meals with low nutritional value.
LanguageData.Fish_Toxfish.label: glowing gulpy
LanguageData.Plant_TreeDrago.description: A hardy desert yucca with twisted limbs and dagger leaves. In the old Mojave, these trees marked roads, water, and trouble from miles away.
LanguageData.Plant_TreeDrago.label: joshua tree
LanguageData.Plant_TreeSnagroot.description: A blackened, near dead tree which survived a nearby atomic blast. When harvested, it yields a meager amount of wood.
LanguageData.Plant_TreeSnagroot.label: charred tree
LanguageData.Ambrosia.description: A rare, mutated fruit found in the wasteland. Punga fruit is prized for its unique taste and the mild, uplifting high it provides. Consuming too much can lead to a subtle addiction, but survivors swear by its mood-boosting effects.
LanguageData.Ambrosia.label: punga fruit
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAnchovy.description: A small silvery bait fish that travels in dense schools through estuaries and coastal shallows.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAnchovy.label: brook silverside
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAngelfish.description: A bright reef fish with tall fins and sharp territorial behavior despite its ornamental look.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAngelfish.label: sunscream
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAnglerfish.description: A deepwater ambush predator that waits motionless in dark pools until prey comes within reach.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawAnglerfish.label: bog lurker
LanguageData.VCEF_RawClownfish.description: A banded saltwater fish once common in pre-war aquariums, now found around warm reef waters.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawClownfish.label: brahfin
LanguageData.VCEF_RawEel.description: A long, slick fish that slips through reeds, drainage pipes, and tidal pools with ease.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawEel.label: purple radpole
LanguageData.VCEF_RawGoldfish.description: A plump ornamental pond fish with bright scales, descended from escaped pre-war stock.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawGoldfish.label: blisterfish
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHaddock.description: A heavy coldwater fish with a rigid back ridge and faintly luminous scales in low light.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHaddock.label: glowing spinefish
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHalibut.description: A huge broad flatfish from deep coastal waters, valued for thick cuts of meat.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHalibut.label: leatherback
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHerring.description: A narrow-bodied shoal fish that runs in fast coldwater schools through inlets and coves.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawHerring.label: timber sawgill
LanguageData.VCEF_RawKoi.description: A glossy freshwater pond fish, likely descended from ornamental carp strains.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawKoi.label: bavarian creekfin
LanguageData.VCEF_RawLobster.description: A small armored crustacean with little meat, usually boiled in batches for stews.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawLobster.label: mirelurk hatchling
LanguageData.VCEF_RawMackerel.description: A fast coastal fish that travels in dense shoals along piers, reefs, and cold surf lines.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawMackerel.label: bavarian tidefin
LanguageData.VCEF_RawMinnow.description: A tiny river fish with a red belly, commonly found schooling in clear creeks.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawMinnow.label: redbelly
LanguageData.VCEF_RawPerch.description: A lean freshwater hunter with yellow eyes, common in cool lakes and slow rivers.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawPerch.label: walleye
LanguageData.VCEF_RawPufferfish.description: A round-bodied fish that inflates when threatened; edible, but not highly prized for taste.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawPufferfish.label: gulpy
LanguageData.VCEF_RawSprat.description: A tiny oily bait fish found in dense cold shoals, commonly netted during seasonal coastal runs.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawSprat.label: alpine sawgill
LanguageData.VCEF_RawSwordfish.description: A fast open-water predator with a long stabbing snout and a powerful strike.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawSwordfish.label: spikesnapper
LanguageData.VCEF_RawTrout.description: A coldwater game fish commonly caught in upland rivers and snowmelt lakes.
LanguageData.VCEF_RawTrout.label: ridge trout
LanguageData.VVE_Lightning.description:
A late-model Corvega supercar tuned for absurd road speed and almost nothing else. It devours gasoline, hates rough ground, and exists mainly to outrun whatever is behind you.
As with most wasteland prestige cars, the speed comes at the cost of fuel economy, durability, and cargo room.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Lightning.label: Corvega lightning
LanguageData.VVE_Highwayman.description:
A rugged Corvega APC rebuilt into an armed road escort. It trades comfort and efficiency for protection, firepower, and reliable highway travel.
It still burns through gasoline at an alarming rate, but wasteland caravans keep them running because they are cheap, fast, and mean enough to scare off light raiders.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Gunner x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Highwayman.label: Corvega APC
LanguageData.VVE_Prowler.description:
A stripped-down Corvega scout buggy built for rough country, bad roads, and quick solo runs beyond the safety of settled routes.
It is cheap, light, and useful in broken terrain, but its exposed frame makes it a poor choice for any real firefight.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Prowler.label: Corvega prowler
LanguageData.VVE_Roadkill.description:
A heavily armored Corvega road tank built around brute force, frontal plating, and simple wasteland survivability.
It is slow, thirsty, and miserable to maintain, but caravans keep one around when they expect the highway to fight back.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Roadkill.label: Corvega roadkill
LanguageData.VVE_Roadrunner.description:
A larger Corvega cargo wagon meant to move serious loads without giving up the speed and road manners of a pre-War automobile.
Compared to older wasteland cars it is more efficient, less visible, and far better suited to long-haul freight runs on maintained roads.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Roadrunner.label: Corvega roadrunner
LanguageData.VVE_Traveller.description:
A lightweight pre-War Chryslus road car built for fast travel, simple cargo hauling, and staying on open pavement whenever possible.
Like most surviving wasteland automobiles, it drinks gasoline greedily and performs worst when dragged into mud, hills, or winter terrain.
<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Traveller.label: chryslus highwayman
LanguageData.MedicineUltratech.label: Vault-Tec medicine
LanguageData.VQED_MilitaryCrateOfDeadlifeShells.descr: A rusted military crate filled with deadlife shells. These artillery rounds are packed with a dust-like compound that settles on nearby human and animal corpses after detonation and raises them as feral ghouls and ghoul-beasts. Many of the shells have lost their potency, but some may still work.
LanguageData.VQE_AncientARCPart.description: An old, rusted fragment of a prototype reactor. It was likely abandoned when Moldaver's construction program was halted. It may still be possible to disassemble it and recover valuable fusion components.
LanguageData.VQE_AncientARCPart.label: ancient reactor part
LanguageData.VQE_Genetron_Basic.description: A prototype fusion core capable of generating large amounts of power from a geothermal foundation. It can later be upgraded into more advanced reactor variants once the correct research notes and hardware have been recovered. Must be placed on a steam geyser.
LanguageData.VQE_Genetron_Basic.label: prototype fusion core
LanguageData.VQE_GenetronJunk.description: A twisted chunk of reactor housing from one of Moldaver's abandoned projects. The valuable parts are gone, and everything else has degraded into scrap.
LanguageData.VQE_GenetronJunk.label: reactor junk
LanguageData.Flake.description: A crumbly dehydrated preparation of brahmin shrooms, dried and pressed into brittle chips. Highly addictive, producing a brief but intense euphoric rush. A common street chem among wasteland settlements.
LanguageData.GoJuice.description: A concentrated distillate extracted from brahmin shrooms under heat and pressure. The resulting compound dramatically sharpens reflexes and dulls pain, making the user temporarily lethal in combat. Highly addictive with severe withdrawal effects.
LanguageData.Luciferium.description: A mysterious pre-war compound recovered from sealed medical bunkers. Steadily enhances all physical and mental capabilities over time. The body becomes permanently dependent — missing a dose triggers a violent and ultimately fatal withdrawal. Nobody knows who made it or why.
LanguageData.Neutroamine.description: Industrial antiseptic compounds stockpiled from pre-war medical and military supplies. Required as a base ingredient for synthesizing most wasteland drugs and medicines. Too concentrated to apply directly.
LanguageData.Penoxycyline.description: Pre-war antibiotics recovered from military medical kits and hospital stockpiles. Prevents common wasteland infections — bloatfly fever, bayou fungus, and the new plague — before they take hold. Must be taken every five days to remain effective.\n\nThis only prevents new infections. It does not treat existing ones, even those not yet symptomatic.
LanguageData.Plant_Psychoid.description: A psychoactive mushroom variety that grows readily in brahmin waste. Mildly hallucinogenic when eaten raw, but primarily cultivated as the base ingredient for a range of stronger preparations.
LanguageData.PsychiteTea.description: A lightly fermented kombucha brewed from brahmin shrooms and water. The fermentation process mellows the psychoactive content, producing a mildly mood-lifting drink with low addiction potential. Widely consumed across settlements as a social beverage.
LanguageData.PsychoidLeaves.description: Freshly harvested brahmin shrooms, still moist and pungent. Mildly psychoactive when eaten raw. Primarily used as the base ingredient for kombucha, dried chips, and more refined chem preparations.
LanguageData.WakeUp.description: A stimulant preparation brewed from herbs, pre-war compounds, and local ingredients following old traditions. Eliminates the need for sleep and sharpens focus for hours. Prolonged use degrades health and creates dependency.
LanguageData.Flake.label: dried brahmin shrooms
LanguageData.GoJuice.label: distilled brahmin shrooms
LanguageData.Luciferium.label: smooch
LanguageData.Neutroamine.label: antiseptics
LanguageData.Penoxycyline.label: antibiotics
LanguageData.Plant_Psychoid.label: brahmin shrooms
LanguageData.PsychiteTea.label: brahmin shroom kombucha
LanguageData.PsychoidLeaves.label: brahmin shrooms
LanguageData.WakeUp.label: voodoo
LanguageData.Yayo.label: fermented brahmin shrooms
LanguageData.Yayo.description: Powdered fermented brahmin shroom preparation, typically snorted for a rapid and intense euphoric effect. Highly addictive and damaging with prolonged use. A potent form of the brahmin shroom product line.
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Gaubricmaker.label: hideweaver treeminder
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Nectarmaker.label: sapbrewer treeminder
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Spitter.label: spitter treeminder
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Stonedigger.label: stonedigger treeminder
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BishopHat.description: A tall ceremonial headdress meant to mark the leader of an organized faith or wasteland cult.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BishopHat.label: high preacher hat
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BrimlessHat.description: A small, plain hat meant to rest gently on one's head. Those who favor it often claim it gives them the look of an old-world mystic.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_CommisarHat.description: A distinguished brimmed cap adorned with a badge of rank, meant to inspire obedience in subordinates at any cost.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_CommisarHat.label: overseer cap
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Fez.description: A flat-topped conical hat decorated with a black tassel, often worn by desert traders, caravan bosses, and other old-road elites.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Rags.description: A set of worn rags meant to cover the body with minimal fuss. They offer almost no protection or warmth, but clearly signal a life of austerity and hard wasteland devotion.
LanguageData.ChoppedStump_Gauranlen.description: The remains of a Treefather, chopped down and left as a stump.
LanguageData.ChoppedStump_Gauranlen.label: chopped Treefather stump
LanguageData.Dryad_Basic.description: A basic treeminder, mammal-like and loyal to its Treefather. It can morph into specialized castes as needed.
LanguageData.Dryad_Basic.label: treeminder
LanguageData.DryadCocoon.description: A cocoon where a treeminder morphs into a new caste, guided by the Treefather's FEV-infused roots.
LanguageData.DryadCocoon.label: treeminder cocoon
LanguageData.DryadHealingPod.description: A pod filled with restorative Treefather sap, used to heal injured treeminders.
LanguageData.DryadHealingPod.label: healing pod
LanguageData.GaumakerCocoon.description: A cocoon where sproutlings merge to create a new Treefather pod, continuing the symbiotic cycle.
LanguageData.GaumakerCocoon.label: sproutling cocoon
LanguageData.GauranlenSeed.description: A seed from the Treefather, able to grow a new mutated tree and begin the symbiotic cycle.
LanguageData.GauranlenSeed.label: Treefather seed
LanguageData.Plant_MossGauranlen.description: A patch of moss that grows around the Treefather, beautifying the area and aiding treeminder production.
LanguageData.Plant_MossGauranlen.label: Treefather moss
LanguageData.Plant_PodGauranlen.description: A pod produced by the Treefather, containing the seeds of new treeminders.
LanguageData.Plant_PodGauranlen.label: Treefather pod
LanguageData.Plant_TreeGauranlen.description: A mutated tree infused with FEV, able to produce treeminders and pods. Its roots run deep, connecting it to the wasteland's secrets.
LanguageData.Plant_TreeGauranlen.label: Treefather
LanguageData.SmashedStump_Gauranlen.description: The remains of a Treefather, smashed and left as a stump.
LanguageData.SmashedStump_Gauranlen.label: smashed Treefather stump
LanguageData.Dryad_Barkskin.description: A hardy caste of treeminder, the radbarkling is covered in thick, bark-like hide infused with radiation. It excels at absorbing damage in combat, protecting its tree and allies. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Barkskin.label: radbarkling
LanguageData.Dryad_Berrymaker.description: A dryad caste specialized in food production, the mutfruitling generates nourishing mutfruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Berrymaker.label: mutfruitling
LanguageData.Dryad_Carrier.description: A helpful caste of treeminder, the portling is specialized in carrying items. Under human influence, it hauls goods where they need to go, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Carrier.label: portling
LanguageData.Dryad_Clawer.description: A fierce caste of treeminder, the scratchling is bred for aggression and damage-dealing attacks. Its oversized claws can tear through most threats, but it is vulnerable to retaliation. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Clawer.label: scratchling
LanguageData.Dryad_Gaumaker.description: A unique caste of treeminder, the sproutling can produce new Gauranlen pods and help reproduce the treefather. Three sproutlings must slowly merge to complete the process. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Gaumaker.label: sproutling
LanguageData.Dryad_Medicinemaker.description: A gentle caste of treeminder, the brocflowerling specializes in producing medicinal herbs. It works with its tree to generate healing plants, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Medicinemaker.label: brocflowerling
LanguageData.Dryad_Woodmaker.description: A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder, the scrapwoodling specializes in producing scrap wood. Together with its Gauranlen tree, it generates wood-like material on an ongoing basis, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Woodmaker.label: scrapwoodling
LanguageData.Dryad_Pungaling.description: A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder which specializes in punga production. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can generate punga fruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.\n\nIn general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.Dryad_Pungaling.label: pungaling treeminder
LanguageData.ShuttleCrashing.label: vertibird (crashing)
LanguageData.TransportPod.description: Automated cargo bots used to deliver people and supplies across the planet.
LanguageData.TransportPod.label: cargo bot
LanguageData.AnimusStone.description: A resonant cryptic stone studied by Whitespring researchers. It increases cryptid meditation focus and is often used in Enclave research facilities.
LanguageData.AnimusStone.label: cryptic stone
LanguageData.ChoppedStump_Anima.description: A stump left behind after a Mothman totem has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.ChoppedStump_Anima.label: chopped Mothman stump
LanguageData.NatureShrine_Large.description: A large shrine for honoring cryptid forces. Whitespring operatives use these to amplify cryptid focus during ritualized study.
LanguageData.NatureShrine_Large.label: large cryptic shrine
LanguageData.NatureShrine_Small.description: A small shrine used by cryptid researchers to focus meditation and study. These shrines strengthen cryptid-focused meditation for Whitespring operatives.
LanguageData.NatureShrine_Small.label: small cryptic shrine
LanguageData.Plant_GrassAnima.description:
Mysterious black eggs with a red glow. Tribal peoples find that these eggs grow around the base of a Mothman totem as they meditate. It seems to reflect some kind of strengthening of the totem's cryptic power.

Wasteland scientists have tried to study the phenomenon, but the eggs fail to hatch when observed too closely.

Animals refuse to eat Mothman eggs. Tribal stories say this is done out of respect and fear.
LanguageData.Plant_GrassAnima.label: Mothman eggs
LanguageData.Plant_TreeAnima.description: A Mothman totem used by Appalachia cultists to focus their meditation and worship.
LanguageData.Plant_TreeAnima.label: Mothman totem
LanguageData.SmashedStump_Anima.description: The remnants of a Mothman totem destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.SmashedStump_Anima.label: smashed Mothman stump
LanguageData.Chemshine.description: A harsh, flammable wasteland liquor distilled from chemfuel. Rotgut dulls pain and misery for a while, but it leaves the drinker soaked in volatile fumes and can turn a bad death into a fireball.
LanguageData.Chemshine.label: rotgut
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyDistillery.description: A gilded distillery of very delicate craftsmanship meant for aging pungashine must into a drinkable form.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyDistillery.label: pungashine still
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMush.description: Sliced and pressed punga fruit ready to ripen toward pungashine.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMush.label: pungashine mash
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMust.description: Fully ripened punga liquor base, ready to be distilled into pungashine.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMust.label: pungashine must
LanguageData.VBE_Cigarettes.description: A small, rolled paper tube stuffed loosely with coyote tobacco chew. It offers a brief euphoria, mild stimulation, and the same stale wasteland smell that keeps smokers coming back.
LanguageData.VBE_Cigars.description: A thick cigar rolled from coyote tobacco chew and finished for a more expensive, more deliberate smoke. It carries a rough desert bitterness under the fancy presentation.
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrink.description: A sharp, fizzy bottle of guarana finished with bitterness and a radioactive aftertaste. It hits hard up front and leaves a crash waiting on the other side.
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrink.label: guarana
LanguageData.VBE_FineCocktail.description: A fine atomic cocktail, mixed precisely from multiple ingredients.
LanguageData.VBE_FineCocktail.label: fine atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_GourmetCocktail.description: A miracle of mixology where every glass, garnish, and stir has been tuned into a gourmet atomic cocktail.
LanguageData.VBE_GourmetCocktail.label: gourmet atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_LavishCocktail.description: A lavish atomic cocktail mixed from a wide variety of ingredients and chilled to perfection before being served.
LanguageData.VBE_LavishCocktail.label: lavish atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tobacco.description: A hardy stand of coyote tobacco suited to the Mojave and other dry wasteland climates. Once cured down, the leaves become coyote tobacco chew for smoking, rolling, or trading.
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tobacco.label: coyote tobacco
LanguageData.VBE_RawCoffee.description: Vacuum-sealed tins of pre-War instant coffee. Bitter, shelf-stable, and still potent enough to anchor every Lucky 38 breakfast service.
LanguageData.VBE_RawCoffee.label: instant coffee
LanguageData.VBE_RawTea.description: Dried pre-War tea leaves stored in a Lucky 38 pantry tin. They keep well and still steep into a proper serviceable cup.
LanguageData.VBE_RawTea.label: dried tea leaves
LanguageData.VBE_RawTobacco.description: A cured pouch of coyote tobacco chew. Dry enough to smoke, roll, or pack into richer blends, with the dusty bite wasteland smokers expect.
LanguageData.VBE_RawTobacco.label: coyote tobacco chew
LanguageData.VBE_SimpleCocktail.description: A simple atomic cocktail quickly mixed from a duo of ingredients.
LanguageData.VBE_SimpleCocktail.label: simple atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_Soda.description: A sweet, fizzy bottle of homebrew sarsaparilla. It is more refreshing than nutritious, but a chilled bottle still feels like a pre-War luxury.
LanguageData.VBE_Soda.label: homebrew sarsaparilla
LanguageData.VBE_SodaFountain.description: An all-in-one homebrew sarsaparilla bottling unit. Syrup is mixed, carbonated, chilled, and dispensed into sterilized bottles for service.
LanguageData.VBE_SodaFountain.label: homebrew sarsaparilla fountain
LanguageData.VBE_SodaSyrup.description: A thick, intensely sweet base for homebrew sarsaparilla. It is meant to be cut, carbonated, and bottled before serving.
LanguageData.VBE_SodaSyrup.label: homebrew sarsaparilla syrup
LanguageData.CashRegister_CashRegister.description: A register desk for lounge service, dining tabs, and house accounts. It does nothing on its own, but it anchors the front-of-house operation.
LanguageData.CashRegister_CashRegister.label: register desk
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineBlue.description: A premium Lucky 38 slot bank built for high-limit play, polished brass, and a hungry house cut.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineBlue.label: lucky 38 high roller reels
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineGreen.description: A House-era slot bank tuned for regular patrons and reliable nightly traffic.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineGreen.label: lucky 38 standard reels
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineRed.description: A reclaimed Vault 21 slot machine with a modest stake and a forgiving house edge.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineRed.label: vault 21 penny slots
LanguageData.VBE_EspressoMachineBuilding.description: A polished Lucky 38 Luxobrew coffee pot rebuilt for high-volume service. It handles espresso pulls, reheating chilled coffee, and tea blends from a single compact station.
LanguageData.VBE_EspressoMachineBuilding.label: luxobrew coffee pot
LanguageData.VBE_MintLeaves.description: Bright carrot flowers used to freshen brewed drinks and garnish wasteland dishes.
LanguageData.VBE_MintLeaves.label: carrot flowers
LanguageData.VBE_MintPlant.description: A stand of carrot flowers used for bright herbal garnish in teas and coffee blends.
LanguageData.VBE_MintPlant.label: carrot flower patch
LanguageData.Chocolate.description: A carefully wrapped cache of pre-War chocolate. Rich, sweet, and still decadent enough to anchor desserts and syrups.
LanguageData.Chocolate.label: preserved chocolate
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.descrip: A rare pre-War medical suspension repackaged for the Lucky 38 kitchen. When folded into food, aqua cura floods the body with emergency repair nanites that can drag a diner back from death.
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.label: aqua cura
LanguageData.VCE_RawSpices.description: A preserved blend of pre-War kitchen spices sealed away in a Lucky 38 pantry tin. Enough survives inside each can to brighten stews, sauces, and grills.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSugar.description: Sealed packets of pre-War sugar recovered from old kitchens and pantry caches. It keeps indefinitely and still delivers the same quick rush of sweetness.
LanguageData.VCE_SmokeleafButter.description: A dense, intoxicating butter churned for indulgent Lucky 38 cooking. It spreads rich, hits hard, and turns any meal into a bad idea with excellent flavor.
LanguageData.VCE_SmokeleafButter.label: brahmin shroom butter
LanguageData.VCEV_CookingBook.description:
A battered cookbook packed with reclaimed recipes, ingredient notes, and pre-War kitchen tricks.
Once read, it will give the reader a new favorite ingredient or meal, then fall apart from one too many hands in the kitchen.
LanguageData.VCEV_CookingBook.label: wasteland cookbook
LanguageData.Gastronomy_Waiting.description: Takes orders and serves meals to patrons in your lounges and dining rooms.
LanguageData.CashRegister_Mech.description: An automated service terminal for mechanoid attendants. From here you can direct dining and front-of-house operations.
LanguageData.CashRegister_Mech.label: autonomous service terminal
LanguageData.SmallHotSpring.description: A carefully worked hot spring built over a steam geyser. Its mineral waters offer meditative relaxation, modest recovery benefits, and a premium bathhouse amenity for up to four pawns at once.
LanguageData.SmallHotSpring.label: resort hot spring
LanguageData.Apparel_ArchoShuttle.description: A bizarre archotech signal flare that calls a vertibird out of nowhere. Whatever intelligence answers it is willing to provide one miraculous extraction.
LanguageData.Apparel_ArchoShuttle.label: vertibird signal flare
LanguageData.Apparel_ArchoShuttle.verbs.0.label: pop signal flare
LanguageData.Apparel_ShuttleComm.description: A single-use field transponder used to request Brotherhood vertibird pickup.
LanguageData.Apparel_ShuttleComm.label: Brotherhood field transponder
LanguageData.Apparel_ShuttleComm.verbs.0.label: call vertibird pickup
LanguageData.Spaceports_Beacon.description: A long-range IFF beacon and traffic-control mast used by Brotherhood cells and trusted allies to advertise a safe vertibird approach, coordinate landings, and broadcast refueling availability.
LanguageData.Spaceports_Beacon.label: Brotherhood flight beacon
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelDispenser.description: A service terminal that sells aviation fuel to Brotherhood vertibirds and other authorized aircraft. It must be placed adjacent to a vertibird pad; once payment is verified, pumps inside the pad draw fuel from tanks on the same power network.
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelDispenser.label: vertibird refueling terminal
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelProcessor.description: An industrial processor that refines volatile feedstock and water into aviation fuel for Brotherhood vertibirds. It runs best with water access and turns surplus chemfuel into saleable flight stock.
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelProcessor.label: aviation fuel processor
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelTank.description: A chilled storage tank for processed aviation fuel. Keep it powered or the stock begins to evaporate away.
LanguageData.Spaceports_FuelTank.label: aviation fuel tank
LanguageData.Spaceports_ShuttleLandingPad.description: A hardened landing pad with guidance lights, fueling hookups, and an encrypted transponder for Brotherhood vertibirds. Keep it powered and clear, and visiting patrols, quartermasters, traders, and allied passengers can touch down here.
LanguageData.Spaceports_ShuttleLandingPad.label: Brotherhood vertibird pad
LanguageData.Spaceports_VisitorSpot.description: Designates a configurable point where visiting Brotherhood personnel, traders, and allied passengers will gather after landing. If no valid point is found, they will remain near their vertibird.
LanguageData.Spaceports_VisitorSpot.label: vertibird mustering point
LanguageData.VCE_Chirashizushi.label: mirelurk bowl (simple)
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedSushi.label: deep fried lurk
LanguageData.VCE_Futomaki.label: futolurk (lavish)
LanguageData.VCE_Gunkanmaki.label: gunkanlurk (gourmet)
LanguageData.VCE_Nigiri.label: murkiri (fine)
LanguageData.VCE_Norimaki.label: norilurk (simple)
LanguageData.VCE_Oshizushi.label: oshilurk (gourmet)
LanguageData.VCE_RawSoybean.description: Tart wasteland cranberries. They can be eaten raw, cooked into preserves, or fermented into a thick table sauce.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSoybean.label: cranberries
LanguageData.VCE_RawWasabi.label: xander root
LanguageData.VCE_Soybean.description: A hardy cranberry bush cultivated for sharp red berries. The fruit cooks down into a rich sauce that stands in for the old-world fermented condiment chain.
LanguageData.VCE_Soybean.label: cranberry bush
LanguageData.VCE_SoyFermenter.label: cranberry sauce vat
LanguageData.VCE_SoySauce.description: A rich cranberry reduction used as a dip, glaze, and salty-sweet finishing sauce for sushi and wasteland seafood.
LanguageData.VCE_SoySauce.label: cranberry sauce
LanguageData.VCE_Temaki.label: temlurk (lavish)
LanguageData.VCE_Uramaki.label: uralurk (fine)
LanguageData.VCE_Wasabi.description: A patch of xander root prized for its pungent bite. When ground into paste it makes a strong condiment for seafood and rice dishes.
LanguageData.VCE_Wasabi.label: xander root patch
LanguageData.VCE_WasabiPaste.description: A sharp paste made from ground xander root and used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other seafood dishes.
LanguageData.VCE_WasabiPaste.label: xander root paste
LanguageData.Therapist.description: Help colonists and patrons work through stress, trauma, and dependency in a private counseling suite.
LanguageData.MedievalVendingMachine.description: A low-tech kiosk for patrons that needs no electricity.
LanguageData.MedievalVendingMachine.label: service kiosk
LanguageData.VendingMachine.description: A pre-War kiosk stocked for patrons, casino traffic, and idle lounge crowds.
LanguageData.AtmosphericHeater.description: A Poseidon home reactor powered by scavenged dark archotechnology. It burns bioferrite to agitate atmospheric gases, raising the outdoor temperature while flooding the area with waste heat.
LanguageData.AtmosphericHeater.label: Poseidon home reactor
LanguageData.BioferriteGenerator.description: An experimental generator that works by pressurizing and igniting bioferrite, then harvesting energy from the resulting heat. The fuel remains bioferrite, but the chassis and control layout read like a harsh Poseidon prototype.
LanguageData.BioferriteShaper.description: A specialized shaping bench that uses heat and electromagnetic stimulation to temporarily coax bioferrite into a pliable state. The material remains bioferrite, but the surrounding hardware fits cleanly into a Fallout-style industrial workshop.
LanguageData.SerumCentrifuge.description: A Med-Tek serum lab that uses scavenged archotechnology to create single-use injectable serums. Clean, expensive, and morally questionable in exactly the right pre-War way.
LanguageData.SerumCentrifuge.label: Med-Tek serum lab
LanguageData.RB_FloorLamp.description: A floor-mounted Wattz lamp that lights an area using electricity. It is less powerful than a standing lamp, but it looks right at home in a retro-futurist sitting room.
LanguageData.Chemfuel.description: A refined liquid fuel suitable for generators, burners, and industrial feed systems. In a saner age Poseidon would have sold it through a smiling subsidiary with a patriotic slogan.
LanguageData.Chemfuel.label: oil
LanguageData.PS_ChemfuelTank.description: An industrial storage tank meant for oil delivered through the pipe network. Tanks can be siphoned for usable fuel as needed, but even light damage can turn leaking vapors into a violent fireball.
LanguageData.PS_ChemfuelTank.label: oil tank
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelDrain.description: A basin meant to feed stored oil into the network and fill connected tanks.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelDrain.label: oil drain
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelPipe.description: Steel pipes designed to move usable oil from deepchem refineries to oil tanks for storage or to oil-fed structures for automatic refilling.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelPipe.label: oil pipe
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelTap.description: A basin meant to hold dispensed oil from storage tanks for later use.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelTap.label: oil tap
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelValve.description: Switches oil flow on or off.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelValve.label: oil valve
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundChemfuelPipe.description: Buried steel pipes designed to move usable oil from deepchem refineries to oil tanks for storage or to oil-fed structures for automatic refilling.
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundChemfuelPipe.label: subterranean oil pipe
LanguageData.Battery.description: A Poseidon reserve battery for holding excess electricity. Charged units remain dangerous when exposed to rain or fire, regardless of what the brochure claims.
LanguageData.BiofuelRefinery.description: A Poseidon refinery that converts biological matter into oil for generators, burners, and industrial storage systems.
LanguageData.BiofuelRefinery.label: oil refinery
LanguageData.ChemfuelPoweredGenerator.description: Produces power by consuming oil. It must be periodically refueled by hand unless your settlement has the industrial habits of an actual Poseidon facility.
LanguageData.ChemfuelPoweredGenerator.label: oil generator
LanguageData.CommsConsole.description: A Wattz communications console for contacting other factions and orbital traders. Big, expensive, and full of humming pre-War electronics.
LanguageData.Cooler.description: An Appalachia Freeze cooling unit that pushes cold air to one side and hot exhaust to the other. It can preserve food or simply make summer tolerable if the rest of the grid holds.
LanguageData.DeepDrill.description: A Corvega deep-crust drilling rig for extracting minerals from far below the surface. Use a ground-penetrating scanner to detect the best seams.
LanguageData.DrugLab.description: A powered industrial chem lab with containers, heaters, and measurement devices for efficient batch processing of drugs and compounds.
LanguageData.ElectricCrematorium.description: A heavy industrial crematorium that vaporizes corpses at extreme temperatures. It is efficient, impersonal, and unmistakably pre-War in temperament.
LanguageData.ElectricSmelter.description: A Corvega reclamation smelter that extracts usable metal from slag and mixed scrap. It consumes a lot of power and throws off serious industrial heat.
LanguageData.ElectricSmelter.label: Corvega reclamation smelter
LanguageData.FabricationBench.description: A Corvega fabrication bench equipped with advanced tools for producing sophisticated machines and components from simpler materials.
LanguageData.FirefoamPopper.description: A Wattz safety device that sprays fire-retardant foam when it detects a blaze. Exactly the kind of sensible utility hardware that survives long after the corporation that sold it is gone.
LanguageData.FlatscreenTelevision.description: A Radiation King flatscreen television with crystal-clear image and rich color. Trust the king for all your TV and radio needs.
LanguageData.GeothermalGenerator.description: A Poseidon geothermal plant that taps deep heat for steady, high-output power. It is exactly the kind of hulking civic infrastructure Poseidon loved to dominate a skyline with.
LanguageData.GeothermalGenerator.label: geothermal plant
LanguageData.Heater.description: A Wattz Electronics heating unit that converts electricity into controlled room heat. It automatically cycles to reach a target temperature.
LanguageData.HiTechResearchBench.description: A Wattz high-tech research bench with computers and electronic measurement equipment. It enables rapid research and more advanced technical projects.
LanguageData.HospitalBed.description: A Med-Tek hospital bed specially designed for clinical care. Adjustable support geometry and integrated instruments improve patient outcomes over ordinary beds.
LanguageData.MegascreenTelevision.description: A huge Radiation King television for settlements that want their entertainment loud, bright, and impossible to ignore.
LanguageData.NutrientPasteDispenser.description: An industrial dispenser that synthesizes edible nutrient paste from feedstocks placed in adjacent hoppers. Efficient, joyless, and exactly the sort of machine a large contractor would call progress.
LanguageData.OrbitalTradeBeacon.description: A Wattz orbital trade beacon used to mark goods for exchange with orbital merchants. Dependable, compact, and built for practical frontier commerce.
LanguageData.PowerConduit.description: A bundle of utility cables for moving power around a settlement. It can be placed under walls and other structures as part of a proper pre-War grid.
LanguageData.SolarGenerator.description: A Poseidon Civilian Helios Two solar panel array for settlement-scale power generation. It delivers its best output under full daylight and clear skies.
LanguageData.SolarGenerator.label: civilian Helios Two
LanguageData.TubeTelevision.description: A Radiation King tube television built around bulky old display hardware and a distinctly pre-War sense of domestic prestige.
LanguageData.VanometricPowerCell.description: A compact power cell that generates free energy forever without conventional fuel. Even by pre-War standards it feels less like engineering and more like a secret Poseidon program nobody was meant to understand.
LanguageData.VitalsMonitor.description: A Med-Tek patient telemetry unit that improves tend quality, surgery success, and recovery when mounted directly beside a hospital bed.
LanguageData.WatermillGenerator.description: A Poseidon hydro unit designed to draw steady power from moving water. Crowded placements create turbulence and reduce output.
LanguageData.WatermillGenerator.label: hydro turbine
LanguageData.WindTurbine.description: A utility-grade wind turbine for civilian settlements. It needs a broad, clear approach to keep airflow stable.
LanguageData.WindTurbine.label: civilian wind turbine
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autofarmer.description: A large Corvega automated field machine that combines sowing and harvesting across a designated outdoor zone. It deposits harvested goods into attached factory hoppers or drops them on the ground if hoppers are not present, and can be set to cycle automatically once most plants in the zone are fully grown.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autofarmer.label: agri-industrial harvester
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedBiofuelRefinery.desc:
A Corvega automated refinery that converts biological matter such as wood and plant stock into chemfuel.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDrillPlatform.descri: An automated Corvega deep extraction rig for pulling stone and buried metals up from the crust. Works machinery needs hoppers for intake and output, throws off serious heat, and can be overclocked at the cost of even greater draw and temperature.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDrillPlatform.label: Corvega drill platform
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMasonrySaw.descripti:
An industrial Corvega masonry saw capable of cutting stone chunks into usable blocks.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMasonrySaw.label: automated masonry line
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedSmelter.description:
A Corvega automated smelter that extracts usable metal from slag chunks, broken-down weapons, apparel, stone chunks, and mechanoid corpses using strong electromagnets and industrial heat.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Conveyor.description: A basic industrial conveyor for moving materials between work cells with minimal labor handling.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Conveyor.label: industrial conveyor
LanguageData.VFEFactory_FactoryHopper.description: Stores feedstock and finished goods for automated Corvega works machinery.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_FactoryHopper.label: works hopper
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorEntrance.d: A buried intake point that lets an industrial conveyor run beneath short stretches of ground before resurfacing.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorExit.descr: A buried discharge point that brings an underground conveyor back to the surface near the next work cell.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorExit.label: buried conveyor discharge
LanguageData.VHGE_GasFloodlight.description: A tall propane floodlight that illuminates a massive area. It is not bright enough for crop growth, but it provides dependable yard lighting when connected to the propane network.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasFloodlight.label: propane floodlight
LanguageData.VHGE_GasLamp.description: A propane-fed standing lamp for utility lighting. It must be connected to the propane network and can be placed safely outdoors.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasLamp.label: propane lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_GasSunLamp.description: An industrial lamp bright enough for crop growth, but it burns a steady supply of propane. It shuts itself off at night.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasSunLamp.label: propane sun lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_GasWallLamp.description: A propane-fed wall light for utility corridors and work rooms. It must be connected to the propane network and can be placed safely outdoors.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasWallLamp.label: propane wall lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_Helixien.label: propane
LanguageData.Facility_IVDrip.description: A Med-Tek intravenous drip stand for rapidly delivering lost fluids to a patient, helping immunity gain and blood loss recovery when placed directly adjacent to a hospital bed or surgical table. Only works for hospital beds or surgical tables; normal beds will not benefit. Attaching more than one stand to the same bed has no effect.
LanguageData.Facility_IVDrip.label: Med-Tek IV stand
LanguageData.Facility_VitalsCentre.description: A large Med-Tek center for monitoring the vitals of several patients at once, improving tend quality, surgery success, and immunity gain when linked to hospital beds or surgical suites.
LanguageData.Facility_VitalsCentre.label: Med-Tek vitals center
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteDripper.description: A bedside Mama Dolce feeding device that delivers nutrient paste directly to bedridden patients when linked to a nutrient vat. Practical, clinical, and just unsettling enough to feel authentic.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteFeeder.description: A compact Corvega feeder that outputs nutrient paste from the pipe network for routine worker consumption.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteGrinder.description: A Mama Dolce nutrient grinder that reduces organic feedstocks into pipeline-ready paste stock. Efficient, impersonal, and very much in the spirit of industrial pre-War food engineering.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteTap.description: A Mama Dolce paste tap that dispenses prepared nutrient meals from the vat network. It is the kind of practical cafeteria hardware a large contractor would install by the hundred.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteVat.description: A Corvega storage vat for holding large volumes of processed nutrient paste. Destroying one makes exactly the kind of industrial mess you would expect.
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedBattery.description: A sealed Poseidon battery unit with far better efficiency than common storage cells. These pre-War housings are rainproof and less prone to catastrophic discharge.
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedSolarGenerator.description: A high-efficiency Poseidon solar array marketed as the Industrial Helios Two, built to pull significantly more charge from direct sunlight than ordinary civilian panels.
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedSolarGenerator.label: industrial Helios Two
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedWatermillGenerator.descripti: A Poseidon hydro unit designed to draw industrial power from moving water. Crowded installations create turbulence and reduce output.
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedWatermillGenerator.label: industrial hydro turbine
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedWindTurbine.description: A pre-War Poseidon utility turbine built for industrial output. It needs broad clear approaches to keep airflow stable and advertises the sort of oversized confidence Poseidon loved to stamp on its public works.
LanguageData.VFE_AdvancedWindTurbine.label: industrial wind turbine
LanguageData.VFE_LargeAdvancedBattery.description: A large sealed Poseidon battery bank built for dependable utility storage. Unlike older packs, these housings withstand rain without exploding.
LanguageData.VFE_LargeBattery.description: A large Poseidon battery bank meant to hold reserve power for later demand. Fully charged units are dangerous when exposed to rain or fire.
LanguageData.VFE_SmallBattery.description: A compact reserve battery for local circuits and small industrial loads. Charged units can still blow when exposed to rain or fire.
LanguageData.VFE_ComponentFabricationBench.descriptio: A powered Corvega assembly bench fitted for turning raw stock into industrial and spacer-grade components.
LanguageData.VFE_ComponentFabricationBench.label: Corvega assembly bench
LanguageData.VFE_FueledSmelter.description: A fuel-fed foundry station for recovering usable metal from slag and scrap items.
LanguageData.VFE_FueledSmelter.label: fueled foundry
LanguageData.VFE_TableButcherElectric.description: A motorized butcher line with a rotary saw for fast, precise carcass breakdown. It consumes a heavy amount of power.
LanguageData.VFE_TableButcherElectric.label: industrial butcher line
LanguageData.VFE_TableDrugLabElectric.description: A powered Corvega chem lab with heaters, vessels, and gauges for efficient batch processing of compounds and drugs.
LanguageData.VFE_TableDrugLabElectric.label: industrial chem lab
LanguageData.VFE_TableMachiningLarge.description: A large Corvega machining platform with advanced tooling and floor space for rapid fabrication of technical equipment. It consumes power.
LanguageData.VFE_TableMachiningLarge.label: Corvega machining platform
LanguageData.VFE_TableSmithyLarge.description: A powered Corvega forge station for weapons, tools, and heavy benchwork. It draws a substantial load from the grid.
LanguageData.VFE_TableSmithyLarge.label: Corvega forge station
LanguageData.VFE_TableStonecutterElectric.description: An industrial masonry saw with powered cutters for turning chunks into building blocks at assembly-line speed.
LanguageData.VFE_TableStonecutterElectric.label: industrial masonry saw
LanguageData.VFE_TableStoveLarge.description: A large industrial cooking line with multiple ovens and burners for rapid meal production under heavy settlement demand.
LanguageData.VFE_TableStoveLarge.label: industrial galley range
LanguageData.VFE_TableTailorLarge.description: A large industrial tailoring bench with powered sewing equipment for rapid clothing output. It can still limp along without power at reduced speed.
LanguageData.VFE_TableTailorLarge.label: industrial tailoring bench
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_LargePollutionPump.descripti: A large Poseidon groundwater reclamation pump that gradually strips pollution from broad terrain and condenses the recovered waste into haulable toxic packs.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_LargePollutionPump.label: Poseidon reclamation pump
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_PollutionFastPump.descriptio: A compact high-draw reclamation pump that clears polluted terrain quickly by burning a heavy amount of grid power and packing the recovered contaminants into toxic wastepacks.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_PollutionFastPump.label: high-draw reclamation pump
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_WasteCrate.description: A sealed industrial locker for holding toxic waste products and reclaimed hazardous packs without letting them leak into the local environment.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_WasteCrate.label: sealed waste locker
LanguageData.UnstablePowerCell.description: An unstable fusion battery built around a permanently active power core. It will run forever until something damages the housing badly enough to turn the whole unit into a catastrophic detonation.
LanguageData.UnstablePowerCell.label: unstable fusion battery
LanguageData.VFES_LargeRepulsor.description: A large ultratech shield projector with the unmistakable feel of a Poseidon black-budget installation. It consumes enormous power while active and can set the surrounding area ablaze if it absorbs too much punishment too quickly.
LanguageData.VFES_SmallRepulsor.description: A compact ultratech shield projector that reads like one of Poseidon's more secretive defense contracts. It throws up a potent energy field when a threat is present and idles at far lower draw the rest of the time.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_AntiAir.description: A specialized Corvega anti-air turret that intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its defensive radius. It cannot engage ground targets, but it looks every bit like a pre-War perimeter solution.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_ArmoredTurret.description: An armored portable turret with Corvega targeting electronics and reinforced plating. Extra armor helps it survive incoming fire, but the control package remains just dumb enough to be dangerous around friendlies.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_AutocannonDouble.description: A twin-barreled autocannon turret with Corvega fire-control electronics. It trades finesse for volume of fire and remains a power-hungry, violently practical defensive platform.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_Flame.description: A portable flamer turret with Corvega control electronics and a brutally simple fuel system. It can engulf enemies and terrain in fire, and it remains as unsafe as any honest pre-War weapon platform should be.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_MilitaryTurret.description: A refined automated turret with Corvega targeting hardware, military-grade stabilization, and hardened casing. Its dumb AI brain still cannot be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_Minigun.description: A heavy automatic turret whose multi-barrel mechanism unleashes sustained fire at high speed. Corvega control systems keep it cycling, but they do not make it subtle.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_Searchlight.description: A Corvega searchlight combining an intensely bright lamp with a mirrored reflector, useful for illuminating and dazzling targets across a defensive perimeter.
LanguageData.VFES_Turret_ShotgunTurret.description: A compact automatic turret fitted with a semi-automatic shotgun and Corvega targeting electronics. Short-ranged, nasty, and still not smart enough to trust completely.
LanguageData.Spacer_OutdoorLamp.description: An outdoor utility lamp with an integrated Poseidon solar collector. It charges by day and provides dependable lighting after dark.
LanguageData.VFES_AirPurifier.description: A compact Wattz Electronics air scrubber that filters smoke, dust, and hazardous particulates while improving room cleanliness and indoor air quality.
LanguageData.VFES_AirPurifier.label: wattz air scrubber
LanguageData.VTE_IndustrialCooler.description: A large Appalachia Freeze wall cooler for serious refrigeration and industrial climate control. Cold air exits one side while hot exhaust is dumped out the other.
LanguageData.VTE_IndustrialHeater.description: A large Wattz industrial heater that fits into a wall and pushes serious utility heat. It can automatically cycle to a target temperature.
LanguageData.VTE_LargeCeilingFan.description: A large Wattz ceiling fan for workshops, warehouses, and other rooms that need stronger air circulation.
LanguageData.VTE_SmallCeilingFan.description: A Wattz ceiling fan for slow, steady air circulation in smaller rooms.
LanguageData.VTE_StandingCooler.description: A freestanding Appalachia Freeze cooling unit for open rooms that need relief from summer heat without full freezer performance.
LanguageData.VTE_WallHeater.description: A Wattz wall heater that fits into a wall and keeps rooms warm without wasting floor space.
LanguageData.VTE_WallMountedCooler.description: An Appalachia Freeze wall-mounted cooler for compact freezer rooms and tight utility spaces.
LanguageData.VTE_WallMountedHeater.description: A compact Wattz wall-mounted heater for utility corridors, workshops, and smaller rooms that still need dependable automatic temperature control.
LanguageData.VTE_WallMountedVent.description: A simple Wattz wall vent for equalizing temperatures between adjacent rooms without opening a passageway.
LanguageData.VGE_AmmoBox_GaussShell.description: A small crate containing heavy uranium shells. Used by gauss artillery to reload. A crate is fed directly into adjacent Hellion artillery when it needs to be rearmed.
LanguageData.VGE_CopilotConsole.description: An auxiliary interface that allows a copilot to assist in launching and piloting a Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.VGE_GaussGun.description: A compact electromagnetic cannon that launches dense uranium slugs at extreme velocities. Designed for local bombardment and medium-range interception, it can also target hostile orbital craft. Requires a linked targeting terminal with an operator to function. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.description: A massive reactor assembly that channels a salvaged reactor core into immense electrical output. Its reinforced coils can handle extreme load, making it one of the most potent power sources available for Hellion systems. Adjacent reactor-core power cells multiply the output by 15% each.
LanguageData.VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.label: reactor-core power coil
LanguageData.VGE_GravhulkEngine.description:
A heavy reactor package built for the largest Hellion frames and orbital support hulls. It sustains an enormous lift field, allowing massive aircraft to rise under their own power while operating near a planetary gravity well.
When launched, anything on Hellion deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map.
These oversized assemblies were intended for pre-War logistics craft and mobile aerospace platforms, and they demand immense fabrication capacity to keep operational.
LanguageData.VGE_GravhulkEngine.label: heavy Hellion reactor
LanguageData.VGE_GravjumperEngine.description:
A compact reactor package adapted for a light Hellion. It generates the lift field needed for rapid vertical launch and short-range atmospheric insertion, but it is not suited to true interplanetary travel.
When launched, anything on Hellion deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map.
Often salvaged from scout craft, courier platforms, and other pre-War aerospace projects, these reactors are valued for their agility and relatively modest support requirements.
LanguageData.VGE_GravjumperEngine.label: light Hellion reactor
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipCapacitor.description: Stores large amounts of electricity for later use. Constructed in Hellion deck plating, Hellion capacitors do not obstruct movement.
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipCapacitor.label: Hellion capacitor
LanguageData.VGE_Gun_PointDefense.description: A rapid-fire gun for point-defense use aboard the Hellion.
LanguageData.VGE_PilotBridge.description: An interface that allows launching and piloting a heavy Hellion. It draws power directly from the heavy Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.VGE_PilotCockpit.description: An interface that allows launching and piloting a light Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.VGE_PointDefenseTurret.description: A specialized defensive turret built exclusively for the Hellion. It intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its protective radius, but cannot engage ground targets. Interception chance scales with the target's velocity.
LanguageData.VGE_SolarPanelling.description: Produces electricity from sunlight. Does not work in darkness or under artificial light. It does not obstruct movement or provide cover, but it can only be constructed on Hellion deck plating.
LanguageData.VGE_TargetingTerminal.description: A control station dedicated to operating a single Hellion artillery piece. Requires a crew member to manually aim and fire the weapon. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.AncientGravReactor.description:
An ancient generator powered by a reactor core. Once capable of producing massive amounts of power, it has now fallen into disrepair. The containment shield is leaking, causing toxic fallout to blanket the whole region.
Hack or destroy the reactor to obtain the core. Be warned, it may explode if damaged.
LanguageData.AncientGravReactor.label: ancient reactor
LanguageData.FuelOptimizer.description:
A control unit that synchronizes the Hellion reactor with its VTOL thrust systems. It reduces the fuel needed for Hellion flight.
One Hellion can support two fuel regulators.
LanguageData.FuelOptimizer.label: fuel regulator
LanguageData.GravAnchor.description:
Prevents the local map from being destroyed during a Hellion launch.
Mooring masts stabilize the launch zone around the aircraft and keep the surrounding settlement intact during departure. They cannot be constructed on the Hellion itself, because doing so would prevent takeoff entirely.
LanguageData.GravAnchor.label: mooring mast
LanguageData.Gravcore.description: A compact pre-War reactor core recovered from prototype Hellion systems and related military hardware. It is useless by itself, but it can be integrated into advanced Hellion support equipment.
LanguageData.Gravcore.label: reactor core
LanguageData.GravcorePowerCell.description: A compact generator built around a salvaged reactor core, adapted to provide steady power for the Hellion's onboard systems.
LanguageData.GravcorePowerCell.label: reactor-core power cell
LanguageData.GravEngine.description:
The pre-War reactor assembly at the heart of the Hellion. It sustains the aircraft's vertical lift envelope and powers the heavy systems needed for long-range atmospheric and orbital operations.
When launched, anything on deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map. Without a mooring mast, launching the Hellion will abandon your current map.
The Hellion was designed as an unfinished military prototype, requiring rare components, specialist fabrication, and a great deal of salvage to keep flying.
LanguageData.GravEngine.label: Hellion reactor
LanguageData.GravFieldExtender.description:
A support assembly used to expand the Hellion's airframe. It increases the amount of deck plating the craft can carry and must be built near the Hellion reactor.
One Hellion can support up to six airframe extenders.
LanguageData.GravFieldExtender.label: airframe extender
LanguageData.GravlitePanel.description:
A lightweight pre-War airframe panel used to assemble and reinforce Hellion components. It is durable, heat-resistant, and ideal for advanced aerospace construction.
Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.
LanguageData.GravlitePanel.label: airframe panel
LanguageData.GravshipHull.description: An airtight armored hull section sized for the Hellion's frame. It seals the craft against vacuum exposure and locks directly into the underlying deck plating, so it can only be built as part of the Hellion.
LanguageData.GravshipHull.label: Hellion hull
LanguageData.GravshipShieldGenerator.description:
A defensive projector that protects the Hellion by generating a short-lived repulsor screen against incoming rounds and mortar fire. Those inside the field can still shoot out.
It must be charged before use. One Hellion can support two shield projectors.
LanguageData.GravshipShieldGenerator.label: Hellion shield projector
LanguageData.LargeThruster.description:
An advanced VTOL thruster with a heat-resistant vacstone liner. It greatly increases the Hellion's operational range. Thrusters require an unobstructed exhaust area free of large buildings and Hellion deck plating.
Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.
One Hellion can support up to six heavy VTOL thrusters.
LanguageData.LargeThruster.label: heavy VTOL thruster
LanguageData.PilotConsole.description: A hardened flight console used to launch and pilot the Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.PilotConsole.label: cockpit console
LanguageData.PilotSubpersonaCore.description:
A low-grade navigation core tuned for Hellion flight. It reduces the chance of an accident when flying the Hellion, though it cannot fully compensate for pilot error.
One Hellion can support up to two navigation cores.
LanguageData.PilotSubpersonaCore.label: navigation core
LanguageData.ShipChunk_Mech.description:
A chunk of robot craft made of steel and salvaged airframe panels. It looks like it was torn free during re-entry or blasted off by heavy weapons.
Deconstruct it to obtain valuable resources.
LanguageData.SignalJammer.description: An electronic countermeasures suite that masks the Hellion's signature and helps it approach heavily defended landing sites.
LanguageData.SignalJammer.label: ECM suite
LanguageData.SmallThruster.description:
A compact VTOL thruster that increases the Hellion's operational range. Thrusters require an unobstructed exhaust area free of large buildings and Hellion deck plating.
One Hellion can support up to four small VTOL thrusters.
LanguageData.SmallThruster.label: small VTOL thruster
LanguageData.RobCo_Assaultron.description: A bipedal combat robot manufactured by RobCo Industries for the United States military. It features an olive drab paint scheme with military markings, two claw arms for melee combat, and a head-mounted laser capable of firing a charged beam at range. Fast and aggressive, the Assaultron was designed for frontline assault roles. Some units were fitted with a stealth field generator, allowing them to cloak mid-approach.
LanguageData.RobCo_Assaultron.label: Assaultron
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronDominator.description: A specialized Assaultron hunter-killer variant built around an integrated stealth-field emitter and a dedicated close-assault blade package. It gives up the standard head laser in exchange for faster cloaked approaches and more dangerous melee engagements.
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronDominator.label: Assaultron Dominator
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronSheepsquatch.description: A heavily modified Assaultron chassis built to resemble the legendary Sheepsquatch cryptid of Appalachian folklore. Larger than a standard Assaultron and fitted with an uprated head laser, it was likely constructed as an intimidation tool by whoever had access to a robotics workbench and a dark sense of humor.
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronSheepsquatch.label: sheepsquatch
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronBlade.description: An integrated Assaultron-pattern forearm blade optimized for rapid slashing and puncturing strikes at close range.
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronBlade.label: Assaultron blade
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronBlade.tools.0.label: Assaultron blade
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronHeadLaser.description: A compact Assaultron-pattern head laser with a faster firing cycle than heavier beam projectors. This makes it more suitable for rapid engagements and close-quarters combat.
LanguageData.RobCo_AssaultronHeadLaser.label: RobCo Assaultron head laser
LanguageData.RobCo_SheepsquatchHeadLaser.description: A high-output head-mounted beam projector built around a long charge cycle and a large blast radius. The discharge produces significant heat and is most effective when fired from standoff range.
LanguageData.RobCo_SheepsquatchHeadLaser.label: RobCo sheepsquatch head laser
LanguageData.RobCo_Cyberdog.description: A military robotic canine developed by RobCo Industries for the United States Army. It combines a preserved dog brain with a mechanical body, granting it canine instincts and a sonic bark weapon for ranged combat. Cyberdogs were deployed as scouts, guard animals, and combat companions alongside military personnel.
LanguageData.RobCo_Cyberdog.label: cyberdog
LanguageData.RobCo_CyberdogSonicBark.description: A short-range acoustic projector used by RobCo security units. It emits a concussive sonic pulse that disrupts mechanical targets without relying on direct physical damage.
LanguageData.RobCo_CyberdogSonicBark.label: sonic bark emitter
LanguageData.RobCo_Eyebot.description: A small hovering surveillance and broadcast drone manufactured by RobCo Industries. It uses whip antennas and integrated loudspeakers to transmit radio signals and pre-recorded broadcasts, and is equipped with advanced facial recognition systems. The Eyebot carries a light laser weapon for self-defense. Widely deployed by the Enclave for propaganda and reconnaissance, it is fast and elusive but extremely fragile in direct combat.
LanguageData.RobCo_Eyebot.label: Eyebot
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotCrashed.description: The remains of a Vertibot — a pre-War military VTOL drone — brought down and no longer capable of flight. Despite its condition, onboard weapon systems and automated defense protocols remain partially active. It will engage anything that enters its perimeter.
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotCrashed.label: crashed vertibot robot
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotDuraframe.description: An enhanced Eyebot built on the experimental Duraframe chassis. It features reinforced internal components for greater durability than a standard unit and carries a more powerful energy weapon. The most documented Duraframe unit, designated ED-E, was fitted with a Tesla cannon and carried sensitive Enclave research data across the wasteland for decades before being recovered.
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotDuraframe.label: Eyebot duraframe
LanguageData.RobCo_EyeOfHydra.description: A compact drone unit spawned and directed by the Hydra command platform. It carries a focused energy blaster and operates under slaved coordination from its parent unit. Individual Eye of Hydra units are lightly armored but attack in numbers from multiple angles simultaneously. Destroying the parent Hydra reverts them to basic autonomous attack protocols.
LanguageData.RobCo_EyeOfHydra.label: Eye of Hydra
LanguageData.RobCo_GutsyHydra.description: A heavily modified Mister Gutsy-based platform designed to function as a drone command hub. The Hydra carries an integrated system for deploying and coordinating Eye of Hydra units, whose combat effectiveness is enhanced by a tactical aura broadcast from the central platform. Its own weapon array is substantial, and destroying it disrupts any drones it has deployed.
LanguageData.RobCo_GutsyHydra.label: Hydra
LanguageData.RobCo_MissNanny.description: A Mister Handy variant designed for childcare and domestic supervision. It is programmed with an extensive behavioral package covering infant care, hazard detection, etiquette, and disciplinary protocols. The Miss Nanny retains a laser arm for household defense and was marketed to pre-War families as a dedicated childcare solution.
LanguageData.RobCo_MissNanny.label: Miss Nanny
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterFarmhand.description: A Mister Handy unit reconfigured for agricultural work. It is equipped with tool arms suited to planting, harvesting, and field maintenance, along with crop monitoring sensors. Sold to pre-War farming operations, the Mister Farmhand retains a laser arm for worksite defense.
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterFarmhand.label: Mister Farmhand
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterGutsy.description: A militarized variant of the Mister Handy platform, commissioned by the United States Army. It replaces civilian tool arms with a plasma caster and flamethrower, and runs the Sergeant Major v1.0 personality package. The Mister Gutsy features heavier armor than a standard Handy and was deployed in combat zones including the Anchorage front.
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterGutsy.label: Mister Gutsy
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterHandy.description: A hovering domestic servant robot produced by General Atomics International in partnership with RobCo Industries. It is equipped with three articulated arms that accept tool attachments including a buzzsaw, laser cutter, and flamethrower, along with three optical stalks. Its neural network allows near-sentient conversation, and a well-maintained unit is capable of autonomous operation for centuries.
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterHandy.label: Mister Handy
LanguageData.RobCo_GutsyHydraArray.tools.0.label: ripper
LanguageData.RobCo_MissNannyBlaster.tools.0.label: manipulator arm
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterGutsyArray.tools.0.label: ripper
LanguageData.RobCo_MisterHandyBlaster.tools.0.label: sawblade
LanguageData.MechanoidTransponder.description: A pre-War transponder by RobCo Industries.
LanguageData.MechanoidTransponder.label: RobCo Transponder
LanguageData.NanostructuringChip.description: The depleted remains of a Sentry Bot fusion cell.
LanguageData.NanostructuringChip.label: depleted Sentry Bot Fusion Core
LanguageData.PowerfocusChip.description: The severed head of a broken Assaultron.
LanguageData.PowerfocusChip.label: scrap Assaultron head
LanguageData.SignalChip.description: Still functional parts of a Mister Handy unit thruster.
LanguageData.SignalChip.label: salvaged Handy thrusters
LanguageData.SubcoreBasic.label: basic Circuitry
LanguageData.SubcoreRegular.label: military-grade Circuitry
LanguageData.UnfinishedSubcore.label: unfinished RobCo Parts
LanguageData.RobCo_Liberator.description: A small quadrupedal combat robot associated with Chinese-aligned factions in the Commonwealth. It is built around a single integrated laser weapon and four mechanical legs for rapid movement over uneven terrain. The Liberator is lightly armored but fast, and is typically encountered in large groups that attack simultaneously from multiple directions.
LanguageData.RobCo_Liberator.label: liberator
LanguageData.RobCo_LiberatorLaser.description: Military-grade laser rifle integrated into a Chinese Liberator combat robot.
LanguageData.RobCo_LiberatorLaser.label: liberator laser rifle
LanguageData.RobCo_LiberatorLaser.tools.0.label: servo hands
LanguageData.RobCo_LibertyPrime.description: A massive pre-War war machine jointly developed by RobCo Industries and General Atomics International for the United States Army. It stands approximately twenty meters tall and is equipped with twin head-mounted energy cannons and a supply of tactical nuclear bombs for destroying fortified positions. Originally designed to liberate Anchorage from Chinese forces, it was never deployed before the Great War due to power supply failures.
LanguageData.RobCo_LibertyPrime.label: Liberty Prime
